reference: Hi and welcome to Healthcare Management. This sounds like typical lumbosacral radiculopathy with nerve compression. You should get lumbar X-rays or an MRI to see if there is a disc bulge or any other spinal disorder. Also, some neurological diseases may need to be considered, but I am sure this is benign. You will have to rest and take painkillers or corticosteroids until symptoms improve. Sometimes surgery may also be necessary. I wish you good health.
text: Hi and welcome to healthcare management. This sounds like typical lumbar sacral radiculopathy with nerve compression. You should get lumbar X-rays or an MRI to see if there is a disc bulge or any other spinal disorder. Also, some neurological diseases may need to be considered, but I am sure this is benign. You will have to rest and take painkillers or corticosteroids until symptoms improve. Sometimes surgery may also be necessary. I wish you good health.
